about_item: Soft padded 36" x 24" x 2" travel bed easily rolls up for storage.
Use it indoors or outdoors, or in a crate or in your SUV.
Waterproof non-skid bottom keeps the bed from sliding.
Water-resistant ripstop nylon is durable. Polyester fill.

summary.text: Customers find the pet bed convenient for traveling, easy to wash, and appreciate its water resistance. The quality is good, though opinions about size are mixed - while some find it suitable for large dogs, others say it's not big enough for larger breeds. Durability and padding receive mixed feedback, with some finding it durable and well-padded, while others report it's not durable and not soft enough. The thickness receives negative feedback, with customers describing it as pretty thin.

insights.title: Portability
insights.summary: Customers appreciate the pet bed's portability, as it rolls up for easy storage and is perfect for car travel or house crates.

examples.snippet: ...It's the perfect travel bed. Rolls up and easy to wipe down. I couldn't be happier with this product!
...May not be big enough for larger dogs. Rolls up nicely and wipes clean easily. Great for car rides or for relaxing on after a long walk.
...This would be great for camping.
Functionality is great. It's smaller than I thought, but portable, rolls up super easy, and goes through the washing machine/dryer without issue....

insights.title: Washability
insights.summary: Customers find the pet bed washable and easy to clean, with one customer noting it goes through the washing machine and dryer without issues.

examples.snippet: ...This bed goes everywhere with us and packs down for easy transport. Easy to clean, doesn’t soak up water and very durable. Get it!
Both our pooch and its human owner love this sturdy, washable and portable bed.
Works perfectly for our pointer mix when we cruise on our boat. Easy to keep clean and stores well!
Used for traveling in the crate. Easy to throw in the washer. Promotes sleep while traveling, and easy to pack when in a hurry.

insights.title: Quality
insights.summary: Customers find the pet bed to be well-made and appreciate its quality, with their dogs loving it.

examples.snippet: great pad. Had to clean it and was impressed with the protection layer. worked great and my dog loves it.
Nice and dog loved it, was smaller than I was expecting, but not too much. Really nice product.
These beds are rugged and high quality. Fabric on top (red) is soft and strong. Fabric on bottom (black) grips the floor and is sturdy....
This is a nice dog bed that rolls up for travel but my dogs just didn’t like it. I have a soft covered cushion one that they like better....

insights.title: Water resistance
insights.summary: Customers appreciate that the pet bed is water resistant, with one customer noting it's more liquid resistant than most similar products.

examples.snippet: We liked how this bed was waterproof but it did not last long at all for our 8 month lab/husky mix. He easily tore fabric and pulled out stuffing.
...The roll up fairly small, are lightweight, waterproof on the bottom and water resistant on top....
...I wouldn't call this bed waterproof, it is water resistant and quick drying. I love that it can roll up, making it easy for storing....
This bed is not waterproof. It is somewhat water resistant but if you spray it with the hose to clean it then it will soak up a ton of water....

insights.title: Size
insights.summary: Customers have mixed opinions about the pet bed's size, with some finding it appropriate for Lab-sized dogs while others note it's not big enough for larger breeds.

examples.snippet: ...It’s unfortunately too small for my 80 lb German Shepherd to be a real bed, but it will be nice as a mat for outdoor activities.
The size is perfect for my dog. The roll up and clip feature is great for travel. The pad is durable and easy to clean.
Functionality is great. It's smaller than I thought, but portable, rolls up super easy, and goes through the washing machine/dryer without issue....
Large mat but for a medium size dog. Anything larger than a lab and they won't fit stretched out. Good quality travel mat.

Eash to rip or puncture, lasted 2 mins before it had holes from the dogs paws

insights.title: Padding
insights.summary: Customers have mixed opinions about the dog bed's padding, with some finding it nice while others say it's not much and not soft.

examples.snippet: ...it's thicker than having a towel on the ground but otherwise not much padding. I love that its easy to roll up and out away and easy to clean....
...She hasn't tried to destroy it, I think because it's not super fluffy. However, it's not completely waterproof, but it held up great in the wash.
...However, the buckles are too short to move from under the bed making it bumpy. It is a relatively thin bed so the buckles are noticeable....

insights.title: Thickness

not as thick as I would of liked to of had. its ok. I like that fact it rolls up and you can snap it shut for storage.

authors_reviews.title: Perfect for car travel or house crate.
authors_reviews.text: Used for traveling in the crate. Easy to throw in the washer. Promotes sleep while traveling, and easy to pack when in a hurry.
authors_reviews.author: EH

authors_reviews.title: Rugged, high quality, very happy with purchase.
authors_reviews.text: These beds are rugged and high quality. Fabric on top (red) is soft and strong. Fabric on bottom (black) grips the floor and is sturdy. This comes with straps/clips to roll up and transport but that was not what I needed. I wanted these for my camper. Honestly the clips underneath really hurt my feet if I stepped on them (accidentally), so I'm sure my babies were not comfortable. They are sewn to the exterior of the black fabric, so I was able to take a seam ripper and gently remove them... causing no damage or holes to the dog bed. I saved the straps and clips for later use -- I'm sure I will find a use for them somewhere. They wash very nicely. It says "line dry" which is easy in South TX, because I just bake them in the 100+ sun outside and they dry nicely here in the heat. Overall, a great dog bed for the camper. Not super thick for my pampered pets, but that's what the human queen sized bed is for!
authors_reviews.author: Amy C

authors_reviews.title: Convenient but could have more padding
authors_reviews.text: Not as thick as I've was expecting, it's thicker than having a towel on the ground but otherwise not much padding. I love that its easy to roll up and out away and easy to clean. Great for laying out on the grass, but not as much on gravel. Our dog is content to lay on it though, which is all that matters!
authors_reviews.author: DH

authors_reviews.title: Perfect for camping!
authors_reviews.text: Is a perfect bed for camping it’s easy to clean and my dog loves it. My mom had one and when we went camping he would only use that one when the other dog wanted to use it so I knew I had to get one and he loves it super worth the money.
authors_reviews.author: Lewis fam

authors_reviews.title: Great travel beds, especially if you have an incontinent dog
authors_reviews.text: I travel a lot and stay in hotels. I purchased two of the Coleman Travel Beds for my 60 lb. Labs. The beds roll up easily/quickly into reasonably sized bundles. Yea! My previous travel beds had to be pushed into a stuff sack, and it was a pain-in-the-you-know-where to get this done, and usually took 1-2 min. per bed. These beds? 15-20 seconds, which includes loosening/tightening the straps. The underside is some sort of rubbery material, which means even of one of my girls has incontinence issues overnight (not infrequent) the hotel carpet stays nice and dry. This is exactly what I was looking for. Finally, an unexpected bonus...both my girls are heavy "nesters," digging in whatever bed they're using until it's a rumpled mess. This bed, because of the rubbery bottom, can't be rumpled. So there's always a full-size surface for the dog to lay on. Someone commented that these beds aren't suitable for Labrador sized dogs. Well, for my Labs they are. I *do* wish they were a bit larger, but they are okay as is.
authors_reviews.author: hopidopi

authors_reviews.title: My cats like it.
authors_reviews.text: I bought one at Big-Lots on sale as they're closing. My cats, who have several beds, took a liking to this one. I also like it because it can get rained on. I haven't moved it to my terrace yet, but that's the plan when it gets a bit warmer.I liked it enough to order a 2nd one from Amazone. Overall, my cats approve of this bed. I don't know if a big dog would find it plush enough or big enough, but for my cats, it's a hit.
authors_reviews.author: E. Lewis

authors_reviews.title: My dog does not like it due to them sticking in her side when ...
authors_reviews.text: There are large buckles that hold it together when it is rolled up that you can feel through the padding when it is rolled out. My dog does not like it due to them sticking in her side when she lays down. I cut the buckles off and she likes it much better. I am giving it three stars because it is pretty well made and rugged enough for camping, and being on the ground, but it would have better if I didn't have to modify it to use it.
authors_reviews.author: alisha

authors_reviews.title: Great travel dog bed!
authors_reviews.text: This is our second one of the beds. We love them and use them often for our dogs. We take them camping with us, or for group dog walks, of we take the dogs to the park or to dinner at an outdoor restaurant, etc. The roll up fairly small, are lightweight, waterproof on the bottom and water resistant on top. They are cushion-y enough for my greyhounds without being bulky. I do wish they made a size just slightly larger, my greyhounds are pretty happy with them. Even my female, who is like the princess and the pea, is happy with these.
authors_reviews.author: Ferretkingdom

other_countries_reviews.title: Great Bed
other_countries_reviews.text: My dog uses this bed all the time. It’s not overly thick, but it does provide a bit of cushioning. It rolls up easy, is quick to wash and dry, and is plenty big enough for my 50lbs poodle. Great for camping, indoor use, or out on a patio.
other_countries_reviews.author: DJL

other_countries_reviews.title: Excellent lightweight travel dog bed
other_countries_reviews.text: Only just received it but my dog loves it. He's 26lbs and it fit well for him. Very lightweight, appears well made, and it's perfect for travelling. A bit too early to rate durability but thus far me and my dog are very happy with this product.
other_countries_reviews.author: MJJE

other_countries_reviews.title: Clips are underneath your dog while they’re laying on it
other_countries_reviews.text: I don’t know where I thought the clips would be, but it’s poor design to have them under the opened mat. The mat is not thick enough for your dog not to feel them while they’re laying on it.
other_countries_reviews.author: Alan Morrison
